parameterGroups.label: Value
parameterGroups.rationale: Single input value that is sent to and received from the global cable.

properties.note: String ID of the global cable slot in the GlobalRoutingManager. Created on demand if it does not exist. Shared across all DspNetworks in the HISE instance.

gaps.question: The global_cable acts as both sender and receiver on the same cable. When one global_cable node sets a value, how does it avoid feedback loops? Is there a recursion guard?
gaps.context: From infrastructure: yes, there is a recursion guard. But the documentation should clarify the bidirectional nature and that setting Value locally broadcasts to all other global_cable nodes on the same Connection.

gaps.question: The base data description is empty. What is a concise, accurate description of this node's purpose?
gaps.context: Need to supply a description for the reference page. From infrastructure docs: it routes a normalised 0..1 control value across networks via the GlobalRoutingManager.

gaps.question: The compiled global_cable supports sendData() for binary data transmission. Is this feature accessible from the interpreted (IDE) global_cable node, or only from C++ compiled code?
gaps.context: If available in the IDE, this is a significant feature to document. From infrastructure: the Cable class supports it, but the IDE GlobalCableNode may not expose it.

gaps.question: Global cables with IDs starting with '/' can be controlled via OSC. Is this documented behaviour or implementation detail? What input/output range mapping is applied?
gaps.context: From infrastructure: OSC support exists. Users may want to use this for external controller integration.

notes: routing.global_cable is a control node (not in audio signal path) that participates in the GlobalRoutingManager cable system. Values are always clamped to 0..1. It acts as both source and target on the cable -- setting Value sends to all other nodes on the same Connection, and receiving a value from another node triggers modulation output. IsFixRuntimeTarget means it uses compile-time hash-based addressing for C++ export. The empty description needs to be filled in. This is the most feature-rich routing node with OSC support and binary data transmission capabilities.
